title: M02 Mastery Check — Variables, Types & Coercion
description: Quick applied check. No looking things up. Answer from memory.

questions.prompt: Fix the bug: for (var i = 0; i < 3; i++) { setTimeout(() => console.log(i), 0) } — it prints 3,3,3.
questions.answer_key: Change var to let. let creates a new binding per iteration, so each closure captures its own i.

questions.prompt: List all 6 falsy values in JavaScript.
questions.answer_key: false, 0, 0n, '' (empty string), null, undefined — also NaN

questions.prompt: const person = { name: 'Alice' }; person.name = 'Bob'; — This throws a TypeError.
questions.explanation: const prevents rebinding the variable. Mutating properties of the object is allowed.

questions.prompt: An API returns price as the string '42.5'. How do you safely add 10 to it?
questions.answer_key: parseFloat('42.5') + 10 or Number('42.5') + 10 — either converts to number first. Do not use '42.5' + 10 which gives '42.510'.
